LAW AND MOTION 302, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT BY DEFT AMERICAN PRESIDENT LINES, ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING (GRANT MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. COURT AGREES THAT DEFENDANT'S RELIANCE ON THE STANDARD INTERROGATORY RESPONSES DOES NOT SHIFT THE BURDEN UNDER SCHEIDING BECAUSE THERE ARE NO SPECIFIC QUESTIONS RE DEFENDANT. THE COURT ALSO AGREES THAT DEFENDANT'S RELIANCE ON PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY DOES NOT SHIFT THE BURDEN BECAUSE PLAINTIFF LACKS PERSONAL KNOWLEDGE RE DECEDENT'S WORKING ENVIRONMENT. SEE CHAKNOVA V. WILBUR-ELLIS CO., (1999) 69 CAL.APP.4TH 962, 976, FN.12. NEVERTHELESS, PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSES TO SPECIAL ROGS. #25 & #27 ARE FACTUALLY DEVOID AND SHIFT THE BURDEN. SEE VILLA V. MCFERRAN, (1995) 35 CAL.APP.4TH 733. SINCE PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PRESENTED SUBSTANTIAL RESPONSIVE EVIDENCE TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT, THE COURT GRANTS SUMMARY JUDGMENT). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (AJR/PB), REPORTER: CAROL A. KAREN, CSR

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-23-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEAR: John P. Mason, Esq. 332-4262 for pltfs & opposing parties; Cheryl L. Brodsky, Esq. 510/267-0112 for deft & moving party American President Lines, Ltd.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling (GRANT MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. COURT AGREES THAT DEFENDANT'S RELIANCE ON THE STANDARD INTERROGATORY RESPONSES DOES NOT SHIFT THE BURDEN UNDER SCHEIDING BECAUSE THERE ARE NO SPECIFIC QUESTIONS RE DEFENDANT. THE COURT ALSO AGREES THAT DEFENDANT'S RELIANCE ON PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY DOES NOT SHIFT THE BURDEN BECAUSE PLAINTIFF LACKS PERSONAL KNOWLEDGE RE DECEDENT'S WORKING ENVIRONMENT. See Chaknova v. Wilbur-Ellis Co., (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 962, 976, fn.12. NEVERTHELESS, PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSES TO SPECIAL ROGS. #25 & #27 ARE FACTUALLY DEVOID AND SHIFT THE BURDEN. See Villa v. McFerran, (1995) 35 Cal.App.4th 733. SINCE PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PRESENTED SUBSTANTIAL RESPONSIVE EVIDENCE TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT, THE COURT GRANTS SUMMARY JUDGMENT). Order signed in open court. (AJR/PB)

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
